title: Parenting Skills Classes * Refugees/Entrants/Asylees - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Parenting Skills Classes * Refugees/Entrants/Asylees 1 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Disabilities Issues Immigrants Refugees/Entrants/Asylees Offender/Ex-Offender Issues Native American Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF AFRICAN REFUGEE IMMIGRANT ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community events such as youth soccer, open gym, tutoring, community-specific workshops (topics include traditions, art, music, history, or language), leadership workshops, and clothing donation drives. Also assists in enrollment in the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) and helps individuals who are job searching, resume building, and with job application form completion. Provides information about rent assistance programs. 7535 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 972133905 Eligibility: Varies by service with a focus on African refugees and immigrant families Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Events: Varies by service Email (971) 430-6193 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AFRICAN REFUGEE IMMIGRANT ORGANIZATION AFRICAN REFUGEE IMMIGRANT ORGANIZATION
